name: always_agrees
code:
proof fn phi_1_always_agrees<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(s: StrictlyOrderedMap<K>, lo: usize, hi: usize, v: EndPoint, ret: (bool, bool))
    requires
        s.valid(),
        0 <= lo <= hi < s.keys@.len(),
        ret.0 == (forall |i| #![auto] lo <= i <= hi ==> s.vals@[i]@ == v@),
        !ret.0 ==> (ret.1 == (s.vals@[hi as int]@ != v@ && forall |i| #![auto] lo <= i < hi ==> s.vals@[i]@ == v@)),
    ensures
        ret.0 == true,
{
}
reason: Would mean the spec is vacuous and values_agree can never report a mismatch

name: single_element_always_matches
code:
proof fn phi_2_single_element_always_matches<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(s: StrictlyOrderedMap<K>, lo: usize, hi: usize, v: EndPoint, ret: (bool, bool))
    requires
        s.valid(),
        lo == hi,
        0 <= lo <= hi < s.keys@.len(),
        ret.0 == (forall |i| #![auto] lo <= i <= hi ==> s.vals@[i]@ == v@),
        !ret.0 ==> (ret.1 == (s.vals@[hi as int]@ != v@ && forall |i| #![auto] lo <= i < hi ==> s.vals@[i]@ == v@)),
    ensures
        ret.0 == true,
{
}
reason: Would mean a single-element range can never detect a mismatch, collapsing the boundary case

name: mismatch_implies_last_only
code:
proof fn phi_3_mismatch_implies_last_only<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(s: StrictlyOrderedMap<K>, lo: usize, hi: usize, v: EndPoint, ret: (bool, bool))
    requires
        s.valid(),
        0 <= lo <= hi < s.keys@.len(),
        ret.0 == (forall |i| #![auto] lo <= i <= hi ==> s.vals@[i]@ == v@),
        !ret.0 ==> (ret.1 == (s.vals@[hi as int]@ != v@ && forall |i| #![auto] lo <= i < hi ==> s.vals@[i]@ == v@)),
    ensures
        !ret.0 ==> ret.1,
{
}
reason: Would mean the spec cannot express an early (before hi) mismatch, losing the ret.1==false distinction

name: both_false_impossible
code:
proof fn phi_4_both_false_impossible<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(s: StrictlyOrderedMap<K>, lo: usize, hi: usize, v: EndPoint, ret: (bool, bool))
    requires
        s.valid(),
        0 <= lo <= hi < s.keys@.len(),
        ret.0 == (forall |i| #![auto] lo <= i <= hi ==> s.vals@[i]@ == v@),
        !ret.0 ==> (ret.1 == (s.vals@[hi as int]@ != v@ && forall |i| #![auto] lo <= i < hi ==> s.vals@[i]@ == v@)),
    ensures
        !(ret.0 == false && ret.1 == false),
{
}
reason: Would mean early-mismatch case (false,false) is unreachable, contradicting the implementation's explicit return path

name: second_true_implies_first
code:
proof fn phi_5_second_true_implies_first<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(s: StrictlyOrderedMap<K>, lo: usize, hi: usize, v: EndPoint, ret: (bool, bool))
    requires
        s.valid(),
        0 <= lo <= hi < s.keys@.len(),
        ret.0 == (forall |i| #![auto] lo <= i <= hi ==> s.vals@[i]@ == v@),
        !ret.0 ==> (ret.1 == (s.vals@[hi as int]@ != v@ && forall |i| #![auto] lo <= i < hi ==> s.vals@[i]@ == v@)),
    ensures
        ret.1 ==> ret.0,
{
}
reason: Would mean ret.1==true can never co-occur with a mismatch, destroying the "only-last-differs" signal
